original_query: SELECT * FROM (SELECT "DEPT"."DEPTNO", "DEPT"."NAME" FROM "DEPT" INNER JOIN (SELECT "DEPTNO" FROM "EMP" GROUP BY "DEPTNO") AS "t0" ON "DEPT"."DEPTNO" = "t0"."DEPTNO") AS "t1" WHERE "t1"."DEPTNO" <= 10
rewritten_query:
SELECT "DEPT"."DEPTNO", "DEPT"."NAME"
FROM "DEPT"
WHERE "DEPT"."DEPTNO" <= 10
AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM "EMP"
    WHERE "EMP"."DEPTNO" = "DEPT"."DEPTNO"
);

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replace the INNER JOIN with a grouped subquery with an EXISTS clause to avoid the expensive grouping operation on the large EMP table
Eliminate the unnecessary derived table (t1) and apply the filter directly in the main query
Move the DEPTNO <= 10 filter to the main query to allow early filtering
